results.title: 01 airborne asphalt lane
results.prompt: Images lock Tina, the dark sedan, and rear-seat identity; Image 2 supplies only open-air flight scale. One intact suburban traffic lane has peeled upward from the street in a single continuous asphalt ribbon, carrying the intact sedan into the evening sky. The ribbon curls high above the houses while remaining connected to the street far below. Tina is clearly visible in the back seat looking down, delighted. Show the underside of the lifted road and a clean empty groove in the street. No flying-car magic, broken road debris, car wings, detached panels, or parachute.

resolved_params.system_prompt: Create one polished, coherent cinematic Peelerz commercial styleframe, never a collage, storyboard, contact sheet, or design sheet. results.title: 02 peeled neighborhood floats driver declines
results.prompt: Image 1 supplies the sedan and driver-world realism; Image 2 supplies only benign peel logic. A whole ordinary suburban neighborhood—houses, lawns, trees, mailboxes, and parked cars—hovers exactly six feet above its empty foundations after shedding one dull outer skin. Fresh saturated color glows from every revealed surface. On the bare street below, the middle-aged rideshare driver stands alone holding the sedan steering wheel, politely declining the adventure as Tina and the car hover overhead. One continuous wide golden-hour photograph. No disaster, rubble, balloons, floating islands, or giant hand.
